articles.title: EIP Service Center
articles.description: This page shows data regarding the CRC screening efforts for EIP.

The table below shows the total number of screens that the CRCs have had since May 3rd, 2023, the total that were eligible to continue screening for the EIP Service Center based on their age (15 years or older), and the percentage of the total.


Total Screened
      Total Eligible by Age
      Percent (%)
    4414
1190
27

Note For those marked as “Eligible by Age”, this only includes when the CRC selects the EIP Service Center screener on the ED General Screen page in REDCap. Therefore, this number eligible may be under-represented if the CRC does not select the EIP Service Center screener for all those above the age of 15 years old.
The CRCs continue with the EIP-specific EPIC pre-screen, looking for various criteria that would indicate that the CRC could approach the patient to gather more information regarding risk and need for EIP action. The graph and table below shows the number of screens that met the various criteria, and the percentage of the total eligible based on age.



The risk groups that the CRCs are looking for during their Epic pre-screen include: African-American males between the ages of 15-29, Women of Color (WOC), and individuals who identify within the transgender or non-binary community.
The percentages shown below are the percent of the total eligible for EIP screening based on age. The only exceptions are the percentage of those HIV+ not in care (which is a percentage of the total identified as HIV+), and the percentage of those positive for Syphilis in the past year (which is a percentage of the total with a STI in the past year).

articles.title: EIP Population
articles.description: The purpose of this page is to show the demographic population data for those individuals that EIP has worked with for various clinical purposes. This page shows the entire EIP popluation data, and the population data for individuals seen at specific sites is shown on those pages listed under the Sites tab.
articles.contents:
Contents
Age Distribution
Sex and Gender Identity
Race and Ethnicity
Insurance Coverage
Populations of Interest

The information on this page only includes data since the start of 2023.
EIP has encountered a total of  1981 unique individuals at all sites.
The demographic information for this EIP population is shown on this page. For further information regarding the population demographics of individuals at particular sites, please refer to those pages listed under the Site tab in the navigation bar.
Age Distribution

Since age is based on the individual’s date of birth (DOB) and the date of their encounter, this can change the individual’s age over time with various EIP encounters. The data here is shown only for 2023, so age distribution can be presented. When this information is combined with historical EIP data, age distribution will be based on encounter-level data, rather than patient-level data.

This first histogram shows the ages of individuals seen by EIP grouped by age categories. The age categories in this histogram were chosen because they are the categories used in both the CTR and EHE reports to ODH. The total numbers of individuals within each category as well as the percent of the total individuals encountered by EIP are shown in the table.






The following histogram shows the complete age distribution of all individuals that EIP has encountered, with the density curve overlying the histogram.



The following two histograms also show the overall age distribution of the EIP population. They also allow the ability to filter and select based on self-reported gender (first histogram) and the self-reported race (second histogram). You can filter the information by selecting/deselecting the gender/race from the legend on the right side of the graph. If multiple categories are selected, the histogram shows the cumulative total for each age among the categories selected. If you just want to see the particular age distribution for a single gender/race, you can deselect all other options.






Sex and Gender Identity
EIP collects an individual’s sex assigned at birth as well as their self-defined gender identity.
Race and Ethnicity
Placeholder for data on race and ethnicity data
Insurance Coverage
Placeholder for data on insurance coverage.
Populations of Interest
Placeholder for data on populations of interest.
